title: Heart failure follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

medication.text: Supports review of functional capacity, fluid status, rhythm, cognition, nutrition, medication, renal function, haemoglobin or iron status, deterioration, monitoring frequency, and support needs.
medication.rationale: The NICE section supports documenting interval symptoms and function, fluid status, rhythm, cognition, nutrition, medicines, renal and hematologic context, deterioration, monitoring frequency, and support needs without generating treatment.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports review of functional capacity, fluid status, rhythm, cognition, nutrition, medication, renal function, haemoglobin or iron status, deterioration, monitoring frequency, and support needs.

source.title: Chronic heart failure in adults: diagnosis and management

referral.text: Supports regular primary-care review, record updates, medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, follow-up planning, and specialist access.
referral.rationale: The primary-care review section supports recording updates, clinician medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, care-plan discussion, follow-up, and specialist access without generating a plan.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports regular primary-care review, record updates, medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, follow-up planning, and specialist access.

source_limitations: Heart-failure phenotype and severity, symptoms, weight and fluid trend, rhythm, blood pressure, function, cognition, nutrition, medicines and doses, adherence, laboratory values, ECG, imaging, device status, deterioration, treatment, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
